source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the monitoring service enabled

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the monitoring service enabled
input_row.description: Metrics help you monitor the health and performance of your clusters. Create a monitoring configuration to forward cluster and app metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. Use the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service observability plug-in to create a monitoring configuration for IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig in your cluster, and use this monitoring configuration to automatically collect and forward metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. With IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig, you can collect cluster and pod metrics, such as the CPU and memory usage of your worker nodes, incoming and outgoing HTTP traffic for your pods, and data about several infrastructure components. In addition, the agent can collect custom application metrics by using either a Prometheus-compatible scraper or a StatsD facade. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is a cloud-native, and container-intelligence management system that you can include as part of your IBM Cloud architecture. Use it to gain operational visibility into the performance and health of your applications, services, and platforms. It offers administrators, DevOps teams and developers full stack telemetry with advanced features to monitor and troubleshoot, define alerts, and design custom dashboards. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is operated by Sysdig in partnership with IBM.
input_row.rationale: Gain operational visibility into the performance and health of your apps and your cluster by deploying a Sysdig agent to your worker nodes. The agent collects pod and cluster metrics, and sends these metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. Get critical Kubernetes and container insights for dynamic microservice monitoring. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig auto-discovers Kubernetes environments providing out-of-the-box dashboards and alerts for clusters, nodes, namespaces, services, deployments, pods and more. A single agent per node dynamically discovers all microservices and auto-collects metrics and events from various sources including Kubernetes, hosts, networks, containers, processes, applications and custom metrics like Prometheus, JMX, and StatsD. Mitigate the impact of abnormal situations with proactive notifications. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig includes alerts and multi-channel notifications that you can use to reduce the impact on your day to day operations and accelerate your reaction and response time to anomalies, downtime, and performance degradation. Notification channels that you can easily configure include email, slack, PagerDuty, Webhooks, OpsGenie, and VictorOps. Impact: Before you provision an instance, consider the following information: • The account owner can create, view, and delete an instance of a service in the IBM Cloud. This user can also grant permissions to other users to work with the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service. • Other IBM Cloud users with administrator or editor permissions can manage the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service in the IBM Cloud. These users must also have platform permissions to create resources within the context of the resource group where they plan to provision the instance. After you provision an instance, you must configure metric sources, enable platform metrics, or both. After the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent is deployed in a metric source, collection and forwarding of metrics to the instance is automatic. The IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent automatically collects and reports on pre-defined metrics. You can configure which metrics to monitor in an environment. You can only configure 1 Sysdig instance in a region to automatically collect platform metrics. Standard pricing for IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig applies.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Monitoring there should be a button to Launch the Sysdig dashboard. If instead the button is labeled Connect , then monitoring is not enabled. From Command Line: 1. Check whether a logging configuration is added to your cluster. If one is added, the output of this command will include the monitoring instance name and ID along with some other information. ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Monitoring click Connect . 6. Select the region and the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service instance that you want to use (or create an instance ), and click Connect . From Command Line: 1. Create the Sysdig monitoring configuration. ibmcloud ob monitoring config create --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> -- instance <Sysdig_instance_name_or_ID> Default Value: IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is not configured by default. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-health#sysdig 2. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Monitoring-with-Sysdig
input_row.impact: Before you provision an instance, consider the following information: • The account owner can create, view, and delete an instance of a service in the IBM Cloud. This user can also grant permissions to other users to work with the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service. • Other IBM Cloud users with administrator or editor permissions can manage the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service in the IBM Cloud. These users must also have platform permissions to create resources within the context of the resource group where they plan to provision the instance. After you provision an instance, you must configure metric sources, enable platform metrics, or both. After the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent is deployed in a metric source, collection and forwarding of metrics to the instance is automatic. The IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent automatically collects and reports on pre-defined metrics. You can configure which metrics to monitor in an environment. You can only configure 1 Sysdig instance in a region to automatically collect platform metrics. Standard pricing for IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig applies.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control evaluates whether IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the monitoring service enabled, specifically IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. The audit process involves checking the cluster's monitoring configuration using both IBM Cloud Console and command-line interface (CLI). The provided audit steps include checking the presence of a monitoring configuration via IBM Cloud CLI, which suggests that these properties are accessible and verifiable through IBM Cloud APIs and CLI commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_sysdig_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud CLI Command:** Use `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` to verify if a monitoring configuration is present for the specified cluster. The output should include the monitoring instance name and ID if it is properly configured.
- **IBM Cloud API Calls:** Utilize the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service API to query cluster configurations and verify the monitoring settings. Specifically, check for the integration of the Sysdig monitoring service.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Iterate over all IBM Cloud Kubernetes clusters within the account or specified resource group.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** Ensure that the Sysdig monitoring instance is configured for the cluster. This can be determined by the presence of the monitoring instance name and ID in the configuration list.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The cluster is configured with a valid Sysdig monitoring instance.
  - **Fail:** The cluster lacks a configured Sysdig monitoring instance or the configuration is missing.

By leveraging the IBM Cloud CLI and relevant APIs, this control can be effectively and accurately evaluated in an automated manner.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using IBM Cloud CLI and IBM Cloud APIs, which allows for programmatic verification of the monitoring configuration.
- **IBM Cloud APIs/CLI:** The assessment correctly identifies that the IBM Cloud CLI and APIs can be used to check the monitoring configuration of IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters. The use of the `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list` command is appropriate for this purpose.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly correct. The use of the IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` is valid for checking the monitoring configuration.
- **IBM Cloud CLI Methods:** The CLI command mentioned is appropriate for the task. However, the assessment could benefit from specifying the exact API endpoints used for verification, which would enhance the technical accuracy.
- **Manual Steps:** Not applicable as the approach is automated.
- **Program Name:** The program name `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_sysdig_monitoring_enabled` is descriptive and follows the convention. It clearly indicates the service (Kubernetes), resource (cluster), and the specific check (Sysdig monitoring enabled).

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment could include a fallback manual verification step or a troubleshooting guide if the automated check fails or returns unexpected results.
- **Alternative IBM Cloud API Methods:** While the CLI command is sufficient, specifying the exact API endpoints, such as the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service API for monitoring configurations, would provide a more comprehensive approach.
- **Better Program Name Suggestion:** The current program name is adequate, but it could be slightly improved for clarity, such as `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_monitoring_sysdig_enabled_check`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and leverages appropriate IBM Cloud tools for automation. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is feasible with the provided IBM Cloud CLI capabilities.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** While the assessment is largely accurate, it would benefit from including specific IBM Cloud API endpoints for a more robust verification process. Additionally, consider adding a troubleshooting guide for scenarios where the automated checks might not yield clear results. This would enhance the reliability and completeness of the audit approach.
